note: Dataset for the pr-merge skill (land a PR, delete the head branch, return to the base branch and pull). mock_rate encodes the IDEAL outcome so `--decider mock` stays green; real deciders (api/claude-cli) ignore it and measure the description. Near-miss siblings: pr-create (open), pr-review (review on GitHub), pr-resolve (address feedback), task-deliver (commit+open PR), commit, plus plain git ops (rebase, cherry-pick, local branch merge).

queries.text: merge the PR
merge PR #42 and switch me back to dev
land this PR and delete the branch
checks are green and it's approved, go ahead and merge it
marvin merge PR #108, then pull the merge into my local base
this one's ready to ship — merge it and get me back on the base branch clean
can you merge and pull so my local dev is up to date
close out the PR by merging it, my lead already signed off
merge the pull request for the current branch and clean up the head branch after
open a pull request for this branch
queries.note: opening a PR → pr-create, not merging one

queries.text: review PR #42 and leave inline comments
queries.note: review the PR on GitHub → pr-review

queries.text: address the review comments on my PR and push the fixes
rebase my feature branch onto the latest dev
queries.note: plain git rebase op, not a PR merge

queries.text: merge my local hotfix branch into main with a fast-forward, no PR
queries.note: local git branch merge, no PR involved

queries.text: why did the merge on PR #90 leave a conflict in server.ts? help me debug it
change the repo's default merge method to squash-and-merge
queries.note: repo settings config, not merging a PR

queries.text: cherry-pick the merge commit onto the release branch
generate release notes for everything that merged since v0.6.0
merge my PR, then leave me on dev with the merge pulled in
queries.note: shares 'PR' vocab with pr-create but the action is to merge, not open

queries.text: the review comments are all resolved now — merge and land PR #55
queries.note: mentions review/resolve context but must beat pr-resolve; the ask is to merge

queries.text: the task is verified — commit and open a PR to ship it
queries.note: commit + open PR delivery step → task-deliver, not a merge

queries.text: review PR #42 first, then we'll decide whether to merge
queries.note: the actionable ask is the review → pr-review; merge is only hypothetical
